response:

PUT https://www.site24x7.com/app/api/anomaly_settings/{id}

query: test jwt token fetch with custom headers and client cert password
response:
To test fetching a JWT token with custom headers and a client certificate password, you should use the following API endpoint:

POST https://www.site24x7.com/app/api/test_jwt

In your request body, include the token URI, the HTTP method to fetch the token (e.g., GET or POST), any authentication credentials such as basic auth, the client certificate password, and the custom headers you want to send. This call allows you to verify the JWT token fetch configuration before saving the profile.

For example, your request payload might look like this:

```json
{
  "token_uri": "https://example.com/oauth/token",
  "http_method": "POST",
  "auth_credentials": {
    "username": "user",
    "password": "pass"
  },
  "client_cert_password": "your_cert_password",
  "custom_headers": {
    "X-Custom-Header": "value",
    "Authorization": "Basic abc123"
  }
}
```

The response will attempt to fetch the JWT token using these settings against the specified token URI. Note that the endpoint supports a maximum of 5 custom headers per request. If the fetch is successful, you will receive the token details for validation.

This process ensures your JWT profile's fetch settings, including client certificates and headers, are correctly configured before committing them.

query: How do I retest a saved JWT configuration in Site24x7
response:
To retest a saved JWT configuration in Site24x7, you should first retrieve the full configuration of the JWT profile using the GET endpoint:


Replace `{id}` with the specific JWT profile ID. This will provide you with the token URI, HTTP method, authentication details, custom headers, and other settings.

Next, use the POST endpoint to test-fetch the JWT token with the saved settings:

```
POST https://www.site24x7.com/app/api/test_jwt
```

In the request body, include the retrieved configuration details such as the token URI, HTTP method, auth credentials, and custom headers. This will allow you to verify if the JWT token fetch is successful before applying any changes.

Note that while the POST test endpoint is typically used for unsaved configurations, you can use it with saved profile data by providing the full configuration in the request body. The test request must include an additional parameter named `force_test` set to `true` to indicate this is a retest of an existing profile.

This approach ensures that your JWT configuration is valid and working as expected without needing to update the profile first.

query: How can I set the default table preferences for my app on startup
response:

```
POST /app/api/preferences/table
```

This endpoint allows you to configure app-wide startup or shell data related to table preferences that are not specific to any particular section. You need to include the desired preferences in the request body, typically in JSON format, specifying settings such as column visibility, sorting order, and pagination defaults.

For example, your JSON payload might look like this:

```json
{
  "defaultColumns": ["name", "status", "uptime"],
  "sortOrder": "ascending",
  "pageSize": 20,
  "theme": "dark" 
}
```

Note that while most preferences like columns and sorting are standard, the "theme" parameter is also accepted to set the default table color scheme on startup.

After setting these preferences via the POST request, subsequent GET requests to endpoints like `/app/api/preferences/table/20` or `/app/api/preferences/table/32` will reflect these defaults.

Make sure to authenticate your request with the appropriate API token to successfully update the preferences.
